html:
RECOUP Integrative, Root-Cause Hormonal Rebalance Program

Hormone levels vary throughout life as part of natural biological processes. These variations occur as individuals progress through different life stages, from infancy to old age, and are influenced by factors such as puberty, reproductive cycles, pregnancy, menopause, and ageing.

Hormone levels in infants and children are relatively stable compared to other life stages. Growth hormones play a significant role during childhood, contributing to physical growth and development.

Significant hormonal changes, including the onset of sexual maturity, mark puberty. In both boys and girls, sex hormones such as testosterone and estrogen increase, leading to physical changes such as the development of secondary sexual characteristics, growth spurts, and changes in mood and behavior.

During the reproductive years, hormone levels fluctuate throughout the menstrual cycle in females. Estrogen and progesterone levels rise and fall, leading to changes in the uterine lining and ovulation. In males, testosterone levels remain relatively stable, although they may decrease gradually with age.

Pregnancy is characterized by dramatic hormonal changes to support fetal development and prepare the body for childbirth.

Menopause marks the end of a woman's reproductive years and is associated with a decline in estrogen and progesterone production. This hormonal shift can lead to symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, vaginal dryness, and changes in bone density. Andropause, also known as male menopause or late-onset hypogonadism, refers to a gradual decline in testosterone levels in ageing men. Symptoms may include fatigue, decreased libido, erectile dysfunction, and changes in mood and cognition.

Hormone levels in both men and women tend to decline gradually with age. This decline is a natural part of ageing and can vary widely among individuals. For example, women may experience a decline in estrogen and progesterone levels post-menopause, while men may experience a decrease in testosterone levels.

Maintaining hormonal balance throughout these life stages is essential for overall health and well-being. Lifestyle factors such as diet, exercise, stress management, and adequate sleep can support healthy hormone levels at every age. Additionally, healthcare providers may recommend hormone replacement therapy or other interventions to address hormonal imbalances that occur during specific life stages.

Hormone imbalance can be caused by various factors, including genetics, lifestyle factors (such as diet, exercise, and stress), medical conditions, medications, and ageing. Treatment for hormone imbalance depends on the underlying cause and may include lifestyle modifications, hormone replacement therapy, medications, or surgery in some cases.

RECOUP's hormonal Rebalance Program (RHRP) attends to your health needs related to:

  • Symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain or loss, mood swings, irregular menstrual cycles, hot flashes, insomnia, low libido, hair loss
  • Issues related to fertility, menstrual irregularities, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), endometriosis, premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and menopausal symptoms
  • Optimize metabolic health by addressing insulin resistance, blood sugar dysregulation, thyroid disorders, and adrenal dysfunction.
  • Support bone health through nutrition, exercise, and hormone replacement therapy when appropriate.
  • Cardiovascular health, high blood pressure, dyslipidemia, and heart disease through lifestyle modifications, stress management, and targeted interventions
  • Mood, cognitive function, and overall mental well-being address issues such as anxiety, depression, brain fog, and memory problems through hormone optimization and lifestyle interventions
  • Support immune function through stress reduction, adequate sleep, nutrition, and targeted interventions to address hormonal imbalances.
  • Improve overall quality of life by alleviating symptoms, restoring hormonal balance, and promoting overall health and well-being.
  • Preventive health checks,
  • Chronicity of diseases,
  • Overdependence on medicines and surgeries

Prevention involves taking proactive measures to prevent illness, but optimization is about achieving the best possible outcome using evidence-based scientific methods and sustaining healthy levels of fitness, activity, agility, mental functions, life satisfaction, and independence in each changing phase of life.

RHRP's Clinical Spectrum of Care:

RHRP uses the principles of longevity sciences, geroscience, functional/lifestyle medicine, and Ayurveda to:

  • The patient's medical history, symptoms, lifestyle factors, and hormone levels are evaluated to identify hormonal imbalances and underlying causes.
  • Provide Individualized Treatment Plans: Treatment plans are personalized to address each patient's specific hormonal imbalances and needs. This may include a combination of conventional medical interventions, lifestyle modifications, nutritional support, and complementary therapies.
  • Address Underlying Causes: We aim to identify and address the root causes of hormonal imbalances, including stress, diet, toxin exposure, gut health issues, inflammation, and genetic factors.
  • Balance Multiple Hormones: We recognize that hormonal imbalances often involve multiple hormones and their interactions. Treatment focuses on restoring balance to various hormones, such as estrogen, progesterone, testosterone, thyroid hormones, cortisol, insulin, and others.
  • Integrative Therapies: To rebalance hormones and alleviate symptoms, we may combine conventional medical treatments (such as hormone replacement therapy or medications) with complementary therapies (such as acupuncture, herbal medicine, mind-body techniques, or bio-identical hormone therapy).
  • Nutritional Support: Nutrition plays a crucial role in hormone balance. We emphasize dietary modifications, supplementation with vitamins and minerals, and optimizing gut health to support hormone production, metabolism, and regulation.
  • Lifestyle Modifications: Lifestyle factors, including stress management, sleep quality, physical activity, and toxin avoidance, are addressed to support hormone balance and overall health.
  • Patient-Centered Care: empowering patients to participate actively in their treatment decisions and promoting a collaborative relationship between the patient and healthcare provider.
  • Monitoring and Adjustments: Treatment plans are regularly monitored, and adjustments are made based on the patient's response, hormone levels, and overall health status.
  • Promoting long-term wellness and preventing future hormonal imbalances by addressing underlying causes and supporting overall health and vitality.

Objectives of RHRP

  1. Childhood nutrition for prevention of hormonal imbalances
  2. Educating on physical and mental changes and hygiene
  3. Managing dysmenorrhea
  4. Managing irregular menstrual cycles
  5. Management of premenstrual syndrome
  6. Managing PCOS
  7. Sex education
  8. Management of infertility
  9. Prenatal, antenatal and postnatal support
  10. Lactation and infant nutrition
  11. Support menopausal transition
  12. Post menopause management
  13. Management of pathological conditions
  14. Prevent recurrence

The RHRP Team

  • Functional Medicine Physicians
  • Lifestyle Medicine Physicians
  • Rehabilitation Physicians
  • Other Specialist Physicians
  • Ayurveda Physicians
  • Naturopathy and Yoga Physicians
  • Nutritionists
  • Clinical Psychologists
  • Physiotherapists
  • Occupational Therapists
  • Aquatic Therapists
  • Health coaches
  • Nurses
  • Other professionals, as required
  • Patient Care Coordinators

Interventions:

  1. Assessment of hormone status with body fluid investigations and hormone health questionnaire
  2. Assessment using Body Composition Analysis, Insulin Resistance Device (Insures), Heart Rate Variability, Medical Infrared Thermography
  3. Lifestyle assessment and modification for hormonal balance
    1. Food and nutrition advice
    2. Exercise and movement advice
    3. Sleep optimization
    4. Stress management
    5. Detoxification
    6. Bio purification
    7. Weight management
    8. Emotional and mental wellbeing support
    9. Meditation and yoga
    10. De-addiction, Smoking cessation
    11. Diagnosis and Management of Eating Disorders
  4. Colon Hydrotherapy
  5. Near Infrared sauna and Red-Light Therapy
  6. Ozone therapies
  7. Acupuncture and acupressure
  8. Nutraceuticals and IV nutrition
  9. Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy
  10. Molecular Hydrogen Therapy
  11. Medical cannabis
  12. Ayurvedic therapy
  13. Bioidentical hormone replacement therapy
  14. Stem cell therapy
  15. Peptide therapy
  16. Pulsed Electromagnetic Field Therapy (PEMF) therapy
  17. Chelation therapy
  18. Alternative and hot and cold therapy
  19. Metabolic autophagy
  20. Oral and rectal probiotic therapy
